speaker: MATT
raw_text: The guard raises his hand and the guards stop. "Very well. Proceed inward." Steps aside, the guards separate and you can see they're all looking a little nervous that they basically had their job done for them. It's not going to do well down the chain of command in the very near future. You guys step into the palace, make your way to the main hallway that leads to the central throne chambers. Uriel is not there at the moment. The guards ask you to wait. You wait about five to ten minutes or so before the doors open again and Uriel is hastily walking in, sitting down on his throne. The rest of the council isn't present for this. It's just him currently, and I believe Tofor Brotorus, the female dragonborn on the council, with you guys. So, as you approach, Uriel sits down, his robes swishing about him. "All right, it hasn't been too long. You requested my presence. And I am here. What do you need?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Again?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We, look, we have a body over this bear! Let us through to Uriel; he's been looking for this man. Super important.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The guards look over at the bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They know this man. They should know this man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You found Riskel Daxio?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of course we did!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right, men!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But don't tell him! It's a surprise. It's a present.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The other guards begin to step forward like, "We'll take it from here."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No no no. No, you won't.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The fuck you will.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We would like to deliver this personally as we don't know if he has any co-conspirators, so it would be best if he didn't leave our immediate vicinity.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And we really want to see his face when he opens it. That's the best part of giving a gift.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Do what you will." He looks up and sees Uriel. "My liege, what would you have of me?" Uriel takes a moment, intensely staring at his face. "For so many years you played me and my family. You played the people of this city, of these lands. You've forsaken the name of your father who plucked you from nothing. How? How can you live with this?" Riskel takes a moment, chuckles to himself, this throaty gurgle as he's still trying to breathe through the blood pooling in the back of his throat. "Life is but a stage of our existence. And when you all will die, I will rise again. For I am his blood."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Someone. I don't care. Wake him up."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I got it! I walk over and I slap the shit out of him.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You may kill him.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he's dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With a resounding slap sound, Riskel sends a spatter of spittle and blood that coasts across the floor and his head shakes. As he looks up at you, you can see his teeth that were once white now glisten with fresh blood across the entirety of his smile.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hello, gorgeous.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Over the next couple hours, you meander a while through Abdar's Promenade, there are a couple dives, but the clientele doesn't seem very jovial.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I feel like my intelligence went down one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's no ruleset for reverse inspiration. But I'm going to start looking into designing that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The entire party's intelligence goes to one.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You get a 1d10 bonus against a roll versus him. It's to knock him down a peg.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's like the fate chips system. It's great. Okay. So who's heading the pub crawl?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Am I heading the pub crawl?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You don't know this town. Who knows this town the best?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: None of us know this town.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, it's technically our town. We should get to know our city, you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right. I'm walking in a direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Go ahead and make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right. I'm going to use a different die because it's funnier that way. That's an eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You can't really do this wrong.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You'd be surprised. (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. You guys get a round of drinks. You look over the events of the previous weeks. You chat amongst yourselves and you meet a few individuals in the establishment and make a few friends. Overall, it's a pretty decent way to finish a rather long day of more work than you had originally anticipated. As the buzz begins to wear off and the evening draws to a close, you guys find yourselves getting a little hungry and this place is pretty much solely a bar experience, as opposed to a straight pub, and it's probably pushing close to 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's get a round, shall we?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Oh, I would say you could buy, Scanlan, but you have no money.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You gave me a thousand gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I did, so you have enough to buy for everyone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm feeling generous. I'll buy us a round.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. You guys get a round of drinks. You look over the events of the previous weeks. You chat amongst yourselves and you meet a few individuals in the establishment and make a few friends. Overall, it's a pretty decent way to finish a rather long day of more work than you had originally anticipated. As the buzz begins to wear off and the evening draws to a close, you guys find yourselves getting a little hungry and this place is pretty much solely a bar experience, as opposed to a straight pub, and it's probably pushing close to 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's get a round, shall we?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Oh, I would say you could buy, Scanlan, but you have no money.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You gave me a thousand gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I did, so you have enough to buy for everyone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm feeling generous. I'll buy us a round.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "At this hour?" And the gentleman who's talking to you now, his chin appears to have been broken at some point in his life so as he talks the jaw swivels from side to side in an awkward, old Disney cartoon way. It's hard to really put into words. His hair is spotty in places, it's thinning in the front, but he's well-dressed. It's one of those things where the outfit does not match the persona. But he looks over to you and, "Well, sir, at this point in the evening, food might be a little hard to procure, unfortunately."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we go home?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No! Guys. The evening is our-- what do they say about things like that? The evening is our oyster. Right? Isn't that what they say?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, close.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No, I said it's pretty close.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, thank you, Pike. Let's stay out all night. Come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Come on, old man!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We can see the sunrise. We never do this. We never take time for ourselves.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no. Keyleth's been day-drinking and night-drinking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I have.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's that girl.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Come on, you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm walking up to the barkeep and I say, Hi!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh god, that's so loud.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We need to keep drinking, but also eating, so where's the closest place to do that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. As you're glancing about and you're talking to the bar, and the rest of you guys are finishing a round of drinks, you are looking around--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, really?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "As far as I know."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Guys, there's got to be like some street vendors. Come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to find the most interesting, boisterous clientele in the bar and I'm going to approach them.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we getting in a fight?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Not yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Go ahead and make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you for that. Yes! That's much better. 20.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So as the drinks continue to flow, you guys swap stories, you converse. The evening is drawing closer to like the 1:30-2:00 in the morning, at this point. Most of the other patrons have filtered out of the bar. The Heroes' Feast, after finishing being consumed, itself has reverted back down to the wooden domino, which is a different enchantment to what you're used to. Your more druidic, divine-based Heroes' Feast you summon out of the actual element. This is almost an implement or a focus he uses for the spell each time. It's a different way of getting the same effect. Interesting to you, not being an arcane caster. But nevertheless, as it winds down a bit, you guys are all finding yourselves settling in.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: If you need any help, count on us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Whitestone. Whitestone. Hmm. Place has been kind of dour as of late."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Up-and-coming. I tell you that much.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Really turning around. Really turning around.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You can turn a coin and do right by the world. They are in need, and that place is going to explode, I'm telling you. Can I get another drink, please?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They're even changing the name, they're making it more hip, it's going to be called WhiSto now. It's great. Just shortening it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Another for her as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's had one drink.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This is great.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: When do we tell her that's a Shirley Temple? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Look, it's been fermenting for a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So hammered off this cherry juice, you guys. (laughs)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The moment you say that, you see his jovial exterior harden for a second, and his eyes close and he goes, "That's a strange name to bring up this late in the evening."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I sit down and talk to Gilmore for a moment?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Of course.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And Keyleth gets up and moves and sits next to Vax.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I go and challenge Grog to a dart game.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Gilmore?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, my dear?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We ran into a really weird thing in Whitestone, and I bet you know some shit about it, because you know shit about everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Weird things in particular, is that what you're insinuating?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Kind of, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right, fair enough."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Vecna?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. "Vecna. He has many names historically. The Archlich. The Whispered One. The Dying King. The Lord of the Rotted Tower. The Undying King. Gods like titles, it seems. A bit arrogant, in my opinion. From what I heard, he was born a mortal. Used his capabilities to attempt to cheat death in every way possible, and then once that was acquired, naturally went on to the next great endeavor: becoming a god. He attempted to ascend six hundred years ago. But in that time his cult expanded across the rebuilding civilization. Nearly achieved it until it seemed that the remnants of good in society went and followed him to his place of worship and thwarted his ritual. He was slain, but as you've seen, when entities of such power get to a certain point, death is but a hurdle. You have enough individuals that believe you're a god, that willpower is strong enough to keep you enduring. So his influence exists and has for many years. I don't know if he's a god per se, not yet, but there are those that treat him as such, and that in itself is dangerous because that means he's probably still out there. And I'm pretty sure he's very interested in finishing what he couldn't before."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He thinks for a second and says, "The only orb of spinning death I'm familiar with is a Sphere of Annihilation, but those are generally a bit larger than what you're describing to me, at the very least."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I feel like maybe they wanted it to get bigger? I don't know. Oh! Oh! There was a hand, and an eye, and--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Those generally follow around the phrase 'Vecna'."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, really?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't know too much other than what I've been told historically, and bits of information that I've picked up through the years. Vecna..." And he thinks for a second, he starts looking about if anyone else is near the table, you guys are off and throwing darts.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm listening in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Pike, I don't know where you are, but I assume you're still--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm listening. I'm listening.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. "Vecna. He has many names historically. The Archlich. The Whispered One. The Dying King. The Lord of the Rotted Tower. The Undying King. Gods like titles, it seems. I don't know if he's a god per se, not yet, but there are those that treat him as such, and that in itself is dangerous because that means he's probably still out there. And I'm pretty sure he's very interested in finishing what he couldn't before."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm listening in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Pike, I don't know where you are, but I assume you're still--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm listening. I'm listening.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It's getting very late. I've only been back a few days and I have a lot of business to attend to in the morning."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I got her. I got her.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I got to-- no, no, no, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, she's got to stay!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Vax! Vax! Hold her hair back! Hold her hair back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We should pick her up and carry her around the bar.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Help her out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I need to stay here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Come on, Keyleth!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll go help her out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We've got her. We've got her.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. What are you doing, Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Wait. Okay, I want to help Keyleth out because she's-- yeah. Go ahead, go ahead, go ahead. Hold on. Go ahead.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so as you guys all--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Pike, tell me a story!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys drag Keyleth outside-- Scanlan? You're staying?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm not going near that shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Vax is staying, and Pike? You're staying?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. As this is happening, Gilmore sits up and begins to finish off his drink. "Ah, it's good to see friends."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You too, Gil.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You aren't going, are you?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It had been a few hours, or probably about an hour since the initial feasting, so she's had enough to maintain the effect. But the gentleman who owns the establishment is probably not going to be very happy in the morning when he has to clean up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit. What kind of shots did you give me, Grog?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The weak stuff. Yeah. I think they call it regret?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Peach schnapps.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Goldschlager.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Goldschlager. Jaeger. Lemon citron vodka.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (groans)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh man, I love this game.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should we go home?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go home. We've left our mark here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Does Keyleth lose the benefits of the Heroes' Feast since she retched it all over the ground?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why would you ask that question?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's such a dick question to ask.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: My body absorbed the process and the calories.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He looks to Shayne. Shayne looks to him, counts in her head. "I think probably at least two hours."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why would you ask that question?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's such a dick question to ask.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: My body absorbed the process and the calories.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It had been a few hours, or probably about an hour since the initial feasting, so she's had enough to maintain the effect. But the gentleman who owns the establishment is probably not going to be very happy in the morning when he has to clean up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I made it outside.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Kick some dirt over her puke.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's just twigs and berries, anyway.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys make your way back to Greyskull Keep. As you approach the front gate of your establishment, you see Cordell and Shayne keeping their evening watch over the gate. Cordell shouts down to the rest of you guys, "You've got guests waiting for you!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How long have they been here?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As Dranzel holds out his mug, he goes, "Now, when we arrived to come play the Winter's Crest festival here in Emon, one of my contacts, we'll say an old client of mine last year in Emon, right around the same time mentioned a peculiar thing. Apparently, a wealthy lord here vanished about a year ago. His estate was sold to a private collector. Apparently, this collector keeps his name off the books, and he couldn't really find out any information about who he was or what his business is, but he acquired the entire estate. People were curious as to what his business was, as the previous individual was rather well known and the circumstances rather dubious in disappearance, but I don't know really how that all went together. Anyway! This older collector was fairly active at this estate until a few months ago. The windows have darkened and stayed as such. No furniture touched, the interior elements unpacked or moved. Just went away. So I'm thinking either this fellow has kicked the final bucket, met his end, or is off on some extended business trip, leaving his acquisition and all of its contents ripe for the picking. Problem is, we can't get into the Cloudtop District. Kent and Kaylie have tried numerous times. Almost got arrested." And you can see, Kaylie gives a nod with her shoulders, shrugging. "Still got a few things to learn, apparently."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Laina, bring around some meats! Wait, we already ate.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We already ate. We're fine. We're so full.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Laina, go back to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) She looks tired. She's like, (whimpering) "More meats? Okay. Is it all right if I go to bed?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Sleep well. Glad you made it home safe." She wanders back off, bags under her eyes. Dr. Dranzel waves you all in. "So, that little proposition that we talked about at the bar earlier today? The old man who didn't need his fortune?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Allow me to elaborate a bit in a more private setting, if you don't mind."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: More private than our house?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "This is the more private."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ah. Cin-cin, everybody. Cin-cin.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that lipstick on your face, Vax?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe it is. I don't know where it would have come from, though. This is a land of magic and mystery. Who knows?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As Dranzel holds out his mug, he goes, "Now, when we arrived to come play the Winter's Crest festival here in Emon, one of my contacts, we'll say an old client of mine last year in Emon, right around the same time mentioned a peculiar thing. Apparently, a wealthy lord here vanished about a year ago. His estate was sold to a private collector. Apparently, this collector keeps his name off the books, and he couldn't really find out any information about who he was or what his business is, but he acquired the entire estate. People were curious as to what his business was, as the previous individual was rather well known and the circumstances rather dubious in disappearance, but I don't know really how that all went together. Anyway! This older collector was fairly active at this estate until a few months ago. The windows have darkened and stayed as such. No furniture touched, the interior elements unpacked or moved. Just went away. So I'm thinking either this fellow has kicked the final bucket, met his end, or is off on some extended business trip, leaving his acquisition and all of its contents ripe for the picking. Problem is, we can't get into the Cloudtop District. Kent and Kaylie have tried numerous times. Almost got arrested." And you can see, Kaylie gives a nod with her shoulders, shrugging. "Still got a few things to learn, apparently."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Laina, bring around some meats! Wait, we already ate.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We already ate. We're fine. We're so full.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Laina, go back to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) She looks tired. She's like, (whimpering) "More meats? Okay. Is it all right if I go to bed?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Sleep well. Glad you made it home safe." She wanders back off, bags under her eyes. Dr. Dranzel waves you all in. "So, that little proposition that we talked about at the bar earlier today? The old man who didn't need his fortune?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Allow me to elaborate a bit in a more private setting, if you don't mind."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: More private than our house?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "This is the more private."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ah. Cin-cin, everybody. Cin-cin.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that lipstick on your face, Vax?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe it is. I don't know where it would have come from, though. This is a land of magic and mystery. Who knows?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's been that week.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can also put some beds out in the workshop which has now been vacated.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Grog, carry some beds around.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How long are you guys staying?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Zed sits up, the large, bearded gentleman. He's like, "I'm happy to sleep here. This is fine by me. Just give me a flat surface." He goes over to one of the tables and--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that Zed the drummer?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he's the drummer.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Why don't you invite the little pickpocket to sleep with you? So you can keep an eye on him. So he doesn't steal stuff. Where are you going, perv?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Where are you going, perv? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's all turn in, shall we?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's turn in!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to stay up and keep an eye on the doors when they go into their rooms and make sure they actually stay-- actually, no, I'm going to go find Jareth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Jarett, you mean?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Jarett.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "My mom gave all her savings to put me up there. Now, I only joined the troop about six months back. It was my third year at the Duke's when the good doctor came through and, well, I seized the opportunity. Now, to be perfectly honest up-front, I've already heard a bit about your tales, Shorthalt. (chuckles) The Charming of the Pauper Prince, your rendition of the Summer Sunder, the Cliffs of Dagger Bay. I've been hoping our paths would cross."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She's that good of a flute player. She asks you about your history and your travels and how you met Dr. Dranzel, and everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I tell her all the best parts, and leave out the naughty bits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Interesting tale."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Kingslayer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right, right."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Leader of the rebellion.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "So you've said, a number of times, aye." She drinks a little bit more of the wine, sets it down. "I guess I should tell you a bit more about myself, then."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, please, I'm fascinated.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, I grew up in Kymal, mostly, before moving to Westrunn to train at the College of the White Duke. Finest of the bards, he was."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, yes he--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Sybil. A fine gnomish lass, it was some years ago, she came across a smooth, silver-tongued flautist who blew through like the wind and disappeared." (gasping)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) Wait--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're not there, Drunky.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're drunk, go to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (wheezing) Cliffs of Dagger Bay! Go ahead!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Dagger Bay's just a place.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) What?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, I'm flattered, and, you have quite talent for-- quite a lot of talent for just been playing with the troop for six months. I was impressed by that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you very much. You know where I've heard most of these stories? My mother, Sybil."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Who?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You say that and her arms tense for a minute, involuntary, trying to pull you closer, then they relax, and she steps away, averting her gaze, keeping her head down. She bends down and picks up her blade. Doesn't sheathe it, just holds it limply at her side. She begins to walk to the door. She opens the door and looks back at you, her eyes finally meeting yours. You can see the wet stains across her cheeks. "You're still a scoundrel. It's what's kept you alive this long. In some ways, it's what's kept me alive this long. I've got a lot to think about. I'm going to go for a walk. (sighs) I need some fresh air." And she closes the door behind her.

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: Oh!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As she holds you there, her tousled, short pixie brown hair, you can feel the wetness across the back of your neck, and you hear her voice trembling in your ear say, "Why? Why can't I do it? All the years building up to this and I have the chance, and I can't do it."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry. I'm sorry, I'm so sorry that you're going through this, and I'm more sorry that you're going through this by yourself, but I think deep down you're a Shorthalt, and there's something that you have that's connected to me and I to you, and perhaps I misread that as some sort of-- ugh-- icky attraction, and I apologize, but I've known for a while now that something is missing in my life, some sort of focus, and some sort of moral compass. Maybe it's just arrived at my doorstep?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I'm happy to take care of this for you." He loads his crossbow and plops himself down at the edge of the hall where he has a view of every single door at the south end of the keep. And he's, like, perched and ready. All right. You guys come to consciousness the next morning, more hungover than others, depending on the individual. Come down, breakfast has already been prepared and waiting. (laughs) Poor Laina's been trying to keep it warm for you, and part of it's already being eaten by your guests, who, half of them fell asleep in the actual dining room. Nevertheless, all of you come down. Scanlan, you eventually come out of your confused reverie. Not a lot of good sleep throughout the evening and Kaylie never came back throughout the night.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're going to get awesome art and a cool package of games and stuff, it'll be really awesome, so congrats. All right, so. Jumping back in. The morning comes around, consciousness comes to all of you, you--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I had, in the night I had gone to find Jarett.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh right, Jarett, sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Just to tell him to keep an eye on the rooms and everything. Because, you know, I'm sure they're all great people, but-- I know they have some shady practices, so. I just want to make sure that--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's my daughter you're talking about, by the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We don't know that yet.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. Cordell and Shayne are now changing over into the next morning staff. As they're getting their stuff together, the morning staff would be Kendrick--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, no. She's a fine player and a fine, upstanding person, and you should take special interest in her. She's a good one. Please, if she continues on with your troupe, take good care of her, all right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course. As long as she keeps bringing in the change and doesn't piss me off, I think we're good."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She might. All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Takes his hat and puts it on, finishes his breakfast.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I check around? Look around the keep and make sure-- I mean in the general area and see if anything's missing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So paranoid.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Seriously, I do not trust these people.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Where's our five-thou?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, where is our five-thou? Did it get delivered?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It never got delivered.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I mean, who are you asking?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm asking the guards at the front if anybody came from the keep, from Uriel.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys make your way towards the Cloudtop District. You're allowed through the gates and you find your way across the central residential area to the far northeast side of the district. This is further away from the palace, within viewing distance from Allura's tower, which still stands over the entirety of the district, like this looming monolith. Currently empty and cold as she's over in Whitestone for this investigation. However, you do come across the familiar site of General Krieg's homestead, the one you infiltrated a little over a year ago. It looks like it's a little dilapidated, but strangely enough, for a person who's purchased this estate, there wasn't much attempt to try and beautify it. The ivy has overgrown the bottom of it. There is still the iron gate around it. It is currently closed and there are no guards posted around it. It looks empty and vacant and dark in the windows. It's about noon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, we should stay, we shouldn't go to the house-- Yeah, let's fucking go!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're keeping things safe. Vox Machina. Woo, let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's tough being the good one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't like them at our keep.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're taking the bear or leaving the bear?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're fucking taking the fucking bear!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's go. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Just a simple question.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's my whole fighting fucking thing is I have a fucking bear. That's the whole point of the class that I took. I keep the bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What kind of classes? Have you been going to night school?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, classes. You know, writing, creative writing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Emon Community College.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I'm learning things, every day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Everyone says words. I don't know what all these words mean. What's a douchebag?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We can remember how to get back to that house, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just skate over that one.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. We have Percy and Vax glance over. You take about five minutes or so paying very close attention to any sort of possible movement or light within the building. Nothing. It is dark. It is motionless. The windows are closed. It's a dark interior and you can't really make any details out of the inside from this point, because it is so closed off. Some of the curtains have actually been drawn shut. But not all of them, a few of the windows are open.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Have a look around?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Go around the back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You guys scoot around to the back side. Anything in particular you're looking for?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Any signs of life?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Anyone around? Watching?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Perception check. For those who wish to look around.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 23 for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: One.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Five.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Glancing about, there are none in your immediate vicinity. You're not that far from the Citrine Garrison, which is the centerpiece of the higher military folk. And they do have their own guard station about. You're about a ten-minute walk from the palace, which has a number of guards throughout, and you do have some patrols that come through the city. But in the middle of the day in the Cloudtop, it's not as pressing a place for guard presence. So you haven't seen any immediately around you. But I'm sure if you wait for the next 15-20 minutes, one will probably come your way.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I like it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I ask you guys, before we do anything, maybe it would be best to tell the guards that we're investigating a disappearance? We've heard things about the house and we need to check inside.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What guards?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I agree.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's obviously shady if we go sneaking in, but if we say, "We're going in the house, because we worry for it," then it's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Should we tell them when we go to the bathroom, too?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're above them in rank.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But the guards have the ability to call higher authority on us and we don't want people--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Are there any guards that we can see right now?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's been there for quite some time, you don't know quite how long.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Good boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Trinket comes sniffing up. (sniffing sounds) Go ahead and make a perception check for Trinket.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. It's too much, it's advantage because of his smell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: True.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wow, those sucked both times.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shut up. Shit, they do. 16?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's good!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Enough to open a door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so Trinket sniffs through, steps into the center of the foyer, pushes past the carpet into the middle of the dining room, and looks up at the table and as soon as you look over, Trinket growls for a moment and you see there on the center of the dining room table is a meal set up. Drink, food, but is completely molded over to the point where parts of it have bloated and spilled off and liquid has-- the putrescence has spread across part of the surface.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oof, I throw the gas mask on.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: But it has been largely untouched for a number of months.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Everyone roll an investigation check. This is investigation.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) 14.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. The three of you going upstairs  you find no further traps, you go through the rooms on the top floor. You find that where previously most of them were empty, a lot of them have been furnished now but most of the furnishings have been untouched, like the house was being set up to be almost like a summer home or a side home, but no signs of life; everything seems to have the same layer of dust you found on the floor in the foyer. There is no real wealth being kept here, 'cause I know that's probably what you're looking for. It looks like whoever had intended to, this collector, hadn't quite gotten to the point of wanting to move any of their fortune, their masses, into the upstairs area.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, you guys step through, it's a short hallway and it leads you into a 20x80 long room, and here you do see a number of tapestries, and there are the ones that were left previously, actually. This room has not really been touched much since you came in when Krieg currently lived here. You can see these tapestries are much older, these ones depict more mythological moments. One of them shows the elemental creation of existence. It's almost like a biblical-type very artistic representation of the creation of the different planes. You've seen it done before--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, smart move.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, make a religion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Looking about and keeping this in mind, you don't see any sort of religious iconography. Any of the artwork, any of the small knickknacks that are throughout the den, nothing here speaks of any sort of religious connotation, or at least whoever has decorated this place did so purely out of visual aesthetic choice. So yeah, nothing catches your attention in this room.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go. Who's going first? Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Sure!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pike then Grog?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Pike, Grog-- what's the rest of the marching order here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Trinket, then me.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Then me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Does Pike get a cloak?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I guess me.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Then Vax's wardrobe rack, then me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, it's got to go behind, I have to pull it behind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do I get that invisibility cloak again?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No. (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: And you've heard that the city has been lorded over and run in a very benevolent way by an individual referred to as J'mon Sa Ord. The city has existed for about 400 years, and since its existence has been run by J'mon Sa Ord. People don't know if he's human, what he is. But he's apparently lived that long.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Not enough to recognize anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Kind of like a god of secrets?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. I'm actually going to ask you guys to roll a history check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What is this, history?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. So, glancing at this, the three of you-- you don't recognize the face at all. And the best that you can recall is there have been tales in a far-off continent called Marquet, which is a largely desert landscape. The few bastions of civilization that exist in this desert, there is one central town, referred to as Ank'Harel. You've never been there. You've heard of it. And it's this little oasis in this center of this vast desert.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Oh! Wait!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's where Riskel was going to go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.text: And you've heard that the city has been lorded over and run in a very benevolent way by an individual referred to as J'mon Sa Ord. raw_text: Okay. So as the orbs that have been rotating around for the past, you know, it's been about ten minutes, they probably would have faded anyway, gently dissipate, you focus your intent on the skull, and this object, while it is not a desecrated object per se, there is an essence inside that does not strike you as moral. There is something about its very nature that does give you pause. You are unable to ascertain the nature, given the fact that you would have to take some time with it to really do so, but you get the feeling that there is some power within it, but probably not the kind that is very fortuitous for someone of your persuasion.

preceding_turns.text: Still, then its left eye is empty.

Something with bladed or clawed appendages has been here and has very viciously or hurriedly attempted to find its way through the rubble.
